title: Werauhia vulcanicola
intro: Werauhia vulcanicola, also known as a type of bromeliad, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and pain, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Werauhia vulcanicola include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Werauhia vulcanicola is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to treat conditions such as arthritis, fever, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Werauhia vulcanicola is a species of flowering plant in the bromeliad family, Bromeliaceae. It is native to the Andes mountain range in South America. The plant has a classification of genus Werauhia and species vulcanicola. Its classification is also often referenced as belonging to the Bromeliaceae family. This classification is based on its physical characteristics and genetic analysis.
